timeline.label: Q1 2026 revenue $11.16B (+7.8% YoY) but operating income fell to $1.35B from $1.69B prior-year quarter; margin mix bears watching.
FY2025 net sales $44.3B (+5.7%); operating income $8.05B; cash from operations $9.57B; long-term debt reduced to $9.9B.
FY2024 reported net income spike ($13.4B) from non-recurring items; normalized owner cash better tracked via operating cash flow.
Continued investment in FreeStyle Libre and cardiovascular portfolio (MitraClip, TriClip, Volt) as primary growth engines per MD&A.
S-3ASR shelf registration filed for flexible debt/equity capital markets access.
Post-pandemic diagnostics normalization; nutrition and devices carry mix shift in segment operating earnings.
Separation of AbbVie (ABBV) completed; Abbott retained devices, diagnostics, nutrition, and established pharmaceuticals.
Alere acquisition closed, expanding rapid diagnostics footprint (historical; still in segment mix).
Debt offering (424B5) during COVID period to fund operations and pandemic diagnostics scale-up.
FY2026 proxy (DEF 14A) documents board composition and executive compensation alignment with long-term TSR.

industry.structure: Global healthcare oligopoly across devices, diagnostics, nutrition, and emerging-market generics; scale R&D, regulatory clearance, and hospital/GPO relationships create high entry barriers.
industry.share_shift: Abbott gains in continuous glucose monitoring (Libre) and structural heart; faces share pressure in infant nutrition and periodic diagnostics normalization post-COVID.
industry.trend: Aging populations, diabetes prevalence, and minimally invasive cardiology favor Abbott's device mix; payer pricing and China volume remain cyclical headwinds.
